subject: Databases
title: Advisory Locks
description: A short lesson to help you apply Advisory Locks.

hook.text: You need a distributed lock across app servers but don't want a separate Redis instance. Advisory locks in Postgres.

buildup.text: Advisory locks are arbitrary locks managed by the database. You define the lock key, not the database.

discovery.text: pg_advisory_lock(12345) grabs a lock. Other sessions calling the same ID wait until you release it.

twist.text: Advisory locks don't lock any actual data. They're pure coordination tools — the meaning is up to your app.

climax.text: Use cases: preventing duplicate cron jobs, serializing rare operations, coordinating migrations.

punchline.text: Advisory locks: app-defined coordination using the database as the lock manager.

quiz.question: What makes advisory locks different from regular row or table locks?
options: They don't lock actual data — the application defines what the lock means
They are automatically applied to every query
Advisory locks are faster than all other lock types
